openQuestions.text: Which storage architecture best fits style retrieval: SQLite plus embeddings, graph, or a hybrid?

resolvedQuestions.text: What schema and indexes preserve style identity, metadata, text, provenance, relationships, and embedding lifecycle?
resolvedQuestions.summary: Normalized style-owned source tables plus derived retrieval, FTS, and vector projections with dual freshness hashes.

resolvedQuestions.text: How should an incremental indexer detect, parse, validate, upsert, tombstone, and rebuild from the flat style folders?
resolvedQuestions.summary: Resumable hash-authoritative indexing with atomic row/generation commits, idempotent vector work, quarantined deletion, staged rebuilds, and pointer rollback.

resolvedQuestions.text: What semantic and structured retrieval API should styles/_engine expose, and how should existing file-walking behavior integrate during migration?
resolvedQuestions.summary: Generation-pinned v2 API with pre-channel eligibility, structured/FTS/vector weighted RRF, channel attribution, stable cursors, guarded hydration, and a reversible v1 adapter.

resolvedQuestions.text: Should flat files or the database be the source of truth, and what migration, rollback, and verification stages minimize risk?
resolvedQuestions.summary: Flat files own content and hydration; published SQLite generations own normal retrieval after staged parity-gated migration, with adapter/pointer rollback.

keyFindings.summary: The current checked-in corpus has 1,290 records, not 1,291.
Style identity is UUID-first while slug remains a separately unique filesystem locator.
The retrieval manifest is a strict denormalized projection with provenance, facets, section pointers, and artifact hashes.
Every query triggers two corpus-wide read-and-hash passes for freshness validation.
Ranking is eligibility-first with deterministic plus disposable FTS5 lexical scoring and bounded cards.
A full-corpus query measured about 6.25 seconds and the current pipeline has no persistent semantic or relationship index.
Use a style-owned versioned schema and migration contract rather than memory_index.
Join source, FTS, and vector projections through stable style identity while retaining UUID and slug roles.
Use trigger-maintained external-content FTS5 with style-specific weighted fields.
Separate embedding status, profile, cache, and vector payload while preserving lexical-only availability.
Use mtime as a hint and a canonical multi-artifact hash as freshness authority.
Keep source, FTS, and vector metadata mutations atomic and in-memory caches rebuildable.
Begin with hard eligibility filters plus attributed FTS/vector fusion and defer memory-specific ranking lanes.
Use normalized relational source state with retrieval documents, FTS, and vectors as derived projections.
Map every manifest field to scalar state, normalized children, ingestion snapshots, or derived views.
All artifacts drive aggregate freshness while canonical JSON, tokens, and DESIGN.md drive semantic retrieval.
Separate the all-artifact aggregate hash from the retrieval-document hash.
Canonical retrieval bytes require versioning, fixed keys, normalization, and deterministic ordering.
Concrete constraints, partial indexes, and external-content FTS triggers passed an SQLite pressure test.
Q2 is answered by the normalized schema and index contract.
Use DISCOVER, VERIFY, PARSE_VALIDATE, COMMIT, VECTOR_DRAIN, and PUBLISH with hash-authoritative freshness.
Atomically upsert normalized source and retrieval documents and advance scan markers only after success.
Key vector work by style, retrieval hash, and profile and CAS-check before publication.
Quarantine absence, tombstone after confirmation, and hard-delete only after retention and rebuild gates.
Publish corpus generations atomically with changed styles and bind readers to one SQLite snapshot.
Full rebuild uses verified staged files and atomic pointer switching while old readers finish.
Durable transaction, queue, and staging evidence makes crash recovery deterministic.
Retain prior generations so rollback is a guarded pointer reversal.
The v2 request separates hard eligibility, structured signals, channel controls, and bounded fusion depth.
Every channel joins the same-generation eligible ID set before top-k retrieval.
Queries, vectors, cards, cursors, and hydration bind one published generation and profile.
Use versioned weighted RRF instead of mixing raw structured, BM25, and cosine scales.
Native responses expose channel health and attribution while adapters preserve legacy card shape.
Use generation-bound keyset cursors over a fixed candidate fusion window.
Hydration tokens are locators and all rights, containment, digest, and byte checks remain authoritative.
Projection failures degrade to healthy channels plus structured SQL, never normal-path corpus walking.
A legacy/shadow/persistent adapter isolates migration and rollback from the no-walk endpoint.
Flat artifacts remain content authority while published SQLite generations become sole normal-query authority.
Migrate through immutable baseline, staged bootstrap, shadow dual reads, canary, query cutover, then scanner retirement.
Separate exact compatibility parity from native-v2 relevance and operational metrics.
Cutover requires source-bound parity reports, health/latency gates, and rebuild/restore/rollback drills.
Rollback reverses adapters or generation pointers and never exports derived rows over files.
Bounded repair differs from staged rebuild; restored checkpoints must reconcile newer source files.
Deletes originate in source files, become confirmed tombstones, then purge after retention and parity gates.
Operator lifecycle must expose status, bootstrap, sync, shadow, checkpoint, cutover, rollback, repair, rebuild, and tombstones.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Use the JSON retrieval manifest as the database
ruledOutDirections.reason: It is fully parsed and regenerated, and FTS is disposable.
Current code prefers UUID and separately validates slug uniqueness.
Its schema is coupled to memory-specific cognition, governance, and retention.
The reference implementation verifies hashes and retry state.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Use one JSON blob per style
ruledOutDirections.reason: It cannot enforce or efficiently query multi-valued facets and tokens.
Generated CSS and raw source create duplicate/noisy semantic churn.
Crashes and inode-bound connections can expose or retain partial state.
Transient filesystem state is insufficient destructive evidence.
Structured, BM25, and cosine score scales are incomparable.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Retain corpus walking as persistent fallback
ruledOutDirections.reason: It recreates the latency and freshness problem.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Make SQLite an editable content authority
ruledOutDirections.reason: It breaks file-backed hydration and creates an unimplemented export contract.
There is no deterministic conflict winner.
